# hrobot_firewall (Resource)

Manages the firewall configuration of a Hetzner Robot server. Hetzner allows at most 10 input rules.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "hrobot_firewall" "example" {
  server_number = 1234567
  status        = "active"
  filter_ipv6   = true
  whitelist_hos = true

  # Rules are evaluated top to bottom. Hetzner allows at most 10 input rules.
  input_rule = [
    {
      name       = "ssh"
      ip_version = "ipv4"
      protocol   = "tcp"
      dst_port   = "22"
      action     = "accept"
    },
    {
      name   = "default-deny"
      action = "discard"
    },
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `server_number` (Number) Server number to attach the firewall configuration to.
- `status` (String) active or disabled.

### Optional

- `filter_ipv6` (Boolean) Apply the rules to IPv6 traffic too.
- `input_rule` (Attributes List) Inbound rules, evaluated top to bottom. Hetzner enforces a maximum of 10. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--input_rule))
- `output_rule` (Attributes List) Outbound rules, evaluated top to bottom. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--output_rule))
- `whitelist_hos` (Boolean) Allow Hetzner online services regardless of rules.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Server number (mirrors `server_number`).
- `port` (String) Switch port the firewall is attached to (informational).

<a id="nestedatt--input_rule"></a>
### Nested Schema for `input_rule`

Required:

- `action` (String) accept or discard.

Optional:

- `dst_ip` (String) Destination CIDR.
- `dst_port` (String) Destination port or range.
- `ip_version` (String) ipv4 or ipv6. Required for input rules; leave empty for output rules to apply to both.
- `name` (String) Rule name.
- `protocol` (String) tcp, udp, icmp, gre, ipip, ah, esp, ipencap.
- `src_ip` (String) Source CIDR.
- `src_port` (String) Source port or range (e.g. 1024-65535).
- `tcp_flags` (String) TCP flags expression (e.g. syn|fin).


<a id="nestedatt--output_rule"></a>
### Nested Schema for `output_rule`

Required:

- `action` (String) accept or discard.

Optional:

- `dst_ip` (String) Destination CIDR.
- `dst_port` (String) Destination port or range.
- `ip_version` (String) ipv4 or ipv6. Required for input rules; leave empty for output rules to apply to both.
- `name` (String) Rule name.
- `protocol` (String) tcp, udp, icmp, gre, ipip, ah, esp, ipencap.
- `src_ip` (String) Source CIDR.
- `src_port` (String) Source port or range (e.g. 1024-65535).
- `tcp_flags` (String) TCP flags expression (e.g. syn|fin).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Firewall configuration is imported by the server number.
terraform import hrobot_firewall.example 1234567
```
